rag_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers or invalid expressions should be resolved by ensuring all referenced features or helpers are declared in the same annex or are visible AADL features.
AGREE guarantees in implementation scopes should not reference subcomponent ports directly; use declared auxiliary variables or existing output features instead.
generic_rules: When a validator reports missing or invalid references, trace the issue to the first unresolved identifier and fix the root cause rather than adding new architecture elements.
Assign statements in AGREE contracts should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE guarantees must be placed in valid scopes and should not reference invalid or undefined identifiers.
Implementation-level contracts should avoid referencing subcomponent ports unless they are properly declared as variables or outputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must be syntactically valid and reference only visible identifiers or declared helpers within the same annex.
Use of 'assign' in AGREE contracts must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture elements to resolve AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct the root cause of unresolved references or invalid expressions.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes that reference subcomponent ports without proper declaration.
